name: Gmail App
description: Rules for Gmail workflows, including finding threads, reading context, drafting replies, sending, forwarding, archiving, deleting, unsubscribing, and handling attachments.
whenToApply: The phase mainly happens in Gmail or Google Workspace mail.
The phase needs to find a thread, read email context, draft a reply, send, forward, archive, delete, unsubscribe, move mail, or verify attachments.
whenNotToApply: Gmail is only a notification source and the real task happens in another app.
The phase only copies information out of email without changing Gmail state.
generationGuidance: Separate drafting from sending; a generated reply is not the same as a sent email.
Require the target thread to be identified by visible subject, sender, date, label, and/or message snippet before acting.
Require user approval before sending, forwarding, deleting, unsubscribing, or batch-moving messages.
After state changes, validate against Gmail-visible state such as draft marker, Sent, label, archive, trash, thread, or attachment names.
hardRequirements: If multiple threads match, stop or ask the user to choose.
Sending or forwarding must validate To, Cc, Bcc, subject, body, and attachments before approval.
Draft-only workflows must not send the email.

runtimeReference.title: Gmail App Rules
runtimeReference.purpose: Gmail-specific execution rules, validation rules, and common email workflow mistakes.
whenToRead: Before executing any phase that reads, drafts, sends, forwards, archives, deletes, or moves Gmail messages.
